title: The Psychology of Avoidance (It's Not Laziness)
description: YouTube episode: one idea, 10 clips, ~50s B-roll + voiceover to ~2:30.
youtube_title: The Psychology of Avoidance (It's Not Laziness)

clips.narration: You are not lazy. Your brain is avoiding a feeling, not a task.
Avoidance often starts as a tiny pause. Step forward, then step back.
You walk toward what matters, then stop at the door.
The alarm rings. Relief wins for five more minutes.
The couch is not rest. It is a hiding place with a remote.
You open the fridge looking for a feeling, not food.
You sit down to work, then freeze. Thinking becomes another delay.
Tired eyes on the notebook. The task is still waiting.
Even small choices get heavy when you are already drained.
Avoidance is not a character flaw. It is a pattern. Name it, then take one small step back in.
